body.title: Add JSON file-per-record loguru sink to DiagnosticBase
body.result: loguru sink in DiagnosticBase.__init__; prune_json_logs(days=30); 39 tests pass
body.description:
Every device that inherits DiagnosticBase should automatically write one JSON file per log record to <log_root>/<device_id>/log/json/. Zero per-device wiring: add one loguru sink in DiagnosticBase.__init__ (or a module-level add() call on the root loguru logger).

Filename: YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-mmm_<logger_name>_<level>.json
Content: {ts, level, logger, message, device_id, extra fields from bound context}
Retention: 30 days — prune_json_logs() called from day-close step 9.5 (analogous to prune_emissions() already wired there).

This is the architectural home for the one-file-per-event operational log pattern. The hand-wired emit() calls in pe_chain/ops/scope_guard (added 2026-05-23) are a stopgap pending this and T-igorbase-migrate-to-diagnosticbase.

**Affected files:** ~/dev/src/python_diagnostic_base_class/diagnostic_base/base.py, diagnostic_base/tagged_logger.py
**Design rules:** none (standalone library, no palace rules apply)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: sink + prune function in python_diagnostic_base_class. Out of scope: wiring into Igor or ADC devices (separate tickets).
**Test plan:** Add test to tests/test_base.py — instantiate DiagnosticBase subclass, call .info(), verify JSON file created in expected location with correct fields.
